office_does: A state senator writes and votes on state laws and budgets, conducts oversight, and represents their legislative district.
race_blurb: Incumbent senator Jesse Salomon faces longtime representative Cindy Ryu and limited-government first-time candidate Ira McBee.

candidates.summary: Ryu is a long-serving representative and former mayor and councilmember emphasizing affordability, housing, business, safety, health care, and consumer protection.
highlights: Brings long state and local elected experience [S1][S2]
Emphasizes housing, health care, and consumer protections [S2]

experience.basis: Ryu has long legislative service and prior mayoral and council experience.

candidates.summary: McBee is a first-time candidate advocating lower taxes, less regulation and government growth, public safety, and parental involvement.
highlights: Supports lower taxes and less government growth [S2]

spending.basis: He explicitly calls for less government growth.

experience.basis: He is a first-time legislative candidate with no documented elected record.

social.basis: He explicitly emphasizes parental involvement in schools.

candidates.summary: Salomon is an incumbent senator and former councilmember focused on housing supply, behavioral health, environmental protection, and reproductive rights.
highlights: Supports housing supply and behavioral-health responses [S2]
Supports environmental protection and reproductive rights [S2]

housing.basis: Salomon explicitly emphasizes increasing housing supply.

safety.basis: He combines public safety with behavioral-health responses.

experience.basis: He is an incumbent senator and former deputy mayor and councilmember.
